design_refs: docs/design/01-foundations.md#2.4 (px token geometry, no fractional scaling added)
note: Regression / precondition guard for WIN-SYS-014. It fail-closed pins the render-method and display-scaling NEUTRALITY invariants the Material file-widget path was designed around, plus the upstream preconditions the accepted E-SC (software Start Center) evidence depends on. It pins upstream infrastructure the Material path relies on; it is NOT a Material implementation of the GPU/RDP/fractional-scale/multi-monitor matrix. It moves NO gate: M stays open and the whole rendering/scaling matrix stays build-bound and unverified. Every pin is source/text evidence only -- no build, pixels, or runtime interaction are claimed (runtime_verified:false). Forbidden tokens are scoped to render-method SELECTION identifiers, never the bare word 'raster', so a benign comment cannot trip the absence pin.

render_agnostic.role: The Material file-widget draw path contains no render-method-selection fork, so GPU-vs-software is purely a build-verification concern (pixel parity), never a source divergence. Absence-assertion; baseline verified clean.

dpi_awareness_pinned.role: The Windows scaling model the px-preserving Material geometry assumes: system-DPI-aware, NOT per-monitor. Under this model Windows bitmap-stretches on fractional/non-primary monitors, which is exactly why the fractional-scale matrix stays build-bound.

dpi_manifest_wired.role: The DPI-aware manifest is really applied to every Executable target via mt.exe -updateresource, so the pinned manifest is not dead configuration.

win_skia_default.role: GPU (Skia) is the Windows rendering default; software raster is the fallback, not the default render path.

skia_settings_surface.role: The Skia settings surface exists with conservative schema defaults; ForceSkiaRaster (the software-raster override) defaults off, so raster is never the schema default.

raster_fallback_exists.role: The software-raster fallback the accepted Start Center evidence runs under really exists (bitmap-rendering, Windows/macOS safe-mode, and ForceSkiaRaster each degrade to RenderRaster) and stays a fallback the caller selects, not the default.

matrix.note: The substantive WIN-SYS-014 scope is entirely build-bound and unverified; this contract pins neutrality/preconditions, not row-scope behaviour. None of B/V/I/A/L/P/C/MATRIX is moved.
build_bound: GPU-vs-software pixel PARITY of the render-agnostic Material composition under Skia/Vulkan vs Skia/raster (source proves no fork; only a build proves equal pixels)
remote-desktop (RDP) session behaviour and graceful Skia degradation (runtime observation; no SM_REMOTESESSION detection in-tree)
fractional/display scaling at 100/125/200% under the system-DPI-aware (bitmap-stretch) manifest (legibility/blur/geometry integrity from captures only)
mixed-DPI multi-monitor placement and OS insets (runtime-only)
safe-mode / bitmap-rendering forced-raster paths exercised for real
